body_txt: They're both constantly playful. They're often the smallest (and cutest) in the family. And they're both totally dependent on their parents for food, water, shelter, and love. With so much in common, it's no wonder dogs and babies are often portrayed as having a close relationship. A typical storyline goes something like this: Bob is out walking his Canine Companion Fido when he meets Alice . Bob and Alice fall in love and get married, and Fido moves with them to their new house. Soon, Fido senses something is different about Alice, and a few months later, Fido is introduced to their first child. They might hit it off right away, or there may be a form of Infant Sibling Jealousy because Fido's humans' primary concern is the baby, but eventually, Fido warms up to the strange new creature and they form a close bond. Often, but not always, Fido is a Big Friendly Dog . A puppy and baby bond is a variation where a bond is formed between the two because of their shared youthful playfulness and curiosity. If he is a Heroic Dog , he will see it as his duty to protect the baby or act as a kind of babysitter. They might also be able to communicate with and understand each other, but be unintelligible to other characters, strengthening this bond further. As the baby gets older, expect the trope to grow into A Boy and His X . Truth in Television . Dogs are known to protect human babies because of their pack instinct, as well as to exhibit jealous behavior when a newborn arrives, and it doesn't take a dog long to realize the small human will spill and drop lots of yummy food. That being said , don’t ever leave a baby alone with a dog, or any other animal for that matter, even if it’s a golden retriever you’ve had for a decade who you think couldn’t hurt a fly. Plenty of pets have been known to harm babies and small children out of the blue. Compare Raised by Wolves .
